The last-round constraint eliminates any strategic value in rejecting this offer to negotiate future terms. Accepting guarantees immediate gains:
- My X holdings would triple from 5 to 15 (300% increase)
- My Y would decrease from 25 to 20 (only 20% reduction)
- Total resources would increase from 30 to 35 (16.7% improvement)
- The resulting balance (X:15/Y:20) represents a more equitable distribution

1. Any counter-proposal would need to offer equal or better value to RED, but they receive Y which I have in surplus - they have no incentive to improve terms
2. No future rounds exist to leverage this rejection for better terms
3. Ending without this trade would leave me with X:5/Y:25 (total 30), worse than accepting

The message should clearly communicate:
1. The strategic necessity of acquiring X (highlighting current shortage)
2. The time-sensitive nature of last-round negotiations
3. The quantitative benefits of the trade (total resource increase)
4. The improved balance post-trade
